name: Fetchium
description: Token-efficient web search, extraction, social research, and AI research as an MCP server. 12 tools: multi-backend search, query-aware content extraction, multi-source research with citations, YouTube analysis, Reddit, and Hacker News search. Rust-native, zero-install via npx.

tools.name: fetchium_search
tools.description: Search the web with multi-backend support (Google, DuckDuckGo, Brave, Bing, Tavily, Serper, Exa, and more). Returns ranked, deduplicated results with scores.

tools.name: fetchium_fetch
tools.description: Fetch and extract clean content from any URL using the 5-layer CEP cascade: CSS selectors → readability → headless JS → PDF → OCR.

tools.name: fetchium_research
tools.description: Conduct multi-source research with citations and evidence tracking. Searches, extracts, ranks, validates, and synthesizes findings with citation chains.

tools.name: fetchium_estimate
tools.description: Estimate the token cost of fetching a URL before spending extraction budget.

tools.name: fetchium_expand
tools.description: Expand a previous result by result_id using Progressive Detail Streaming tiers.

tools.name: youtube_search
tools.description: Search YouTube videos with VideoFusion ranking across relevance, freshness, authority, engagement, and educational scores.

tools.name: youtube_analyze
tools.description: Analyze one YouTube video with metadata, transcript, comments, credibility, clickbait detection, and educational scoring.

tools.name: youtube_watch
tools.description: Produce a unified YouTube watch report with metadata, transcript, summary, key moments, and comment signals.

tools.name: youtube_transcript
tools.description: Extract a YouTube transcript with key moments and transcript quality scoring.

tools.name: social_research
tools.description: Run cross-platform social research across Twitter/X, Reddit, TikTok, Hacker News, and YouTube.

tools.name: reddit_search
tools.description: Search Reddit posts with sentiment analysis, subreddit clustering, and viral detection.

tools.name: hackernews_search
tools.description: Search Hacker News stories via Algolia and Firebase APIs, returning ranked stories with engagement metrics.
